messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agent host - "Branch Changes" does not show the branch information ISSUE BODY: Testing #316784 --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host unit tests For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host system-prompt customization This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host** (ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's `extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Locations | Path | Scope | |------|-------| | `.github/agents/*.agent.md` | Workspace | | `/agents/*.agent.md` | User profile | --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### 7.4 Tests - [ ] `src/vs/platform/agentHost/test/node/claudeAgent.test.ts` exists with all 13 cases from §5. - [ ] `ensureNoDisposablesAreLeakedInTestSuite()` is at the top of the suite. - [ ] `scripts/test.sh --grep ClaudeAgent` exits 0 — every case passes. - [ ] No test uses `as any` or `as unknown as Foo` casts. - [ ] No test depends on real network or real subprocesses.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agent host - 'Branch Changes' does not show branch information",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "ui-bug", "display-issue"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a Copilot agent host user, I want to see branch information displayed in the 'Branch Changes' section so that I can understand which branches have been modified during the current session.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an active agent host session with branch changes detected, When viewing the interface, Then the 'Branch Changes' section displays all affected branches",
    "Given no branch changes occurred, When viewing the interface, Then the 'Branch Changes' section shows a clear indicator that there are no changes",
    "The branch information should include: branch name, change description, and timestamp of when changes were detected"
  ],
  "story_points": null,
  "tasks": {},
  "definition_of_done": ["Bug fixed in production environment", "UI renders correctly for all scenarios"],
  "planning_status": "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected source of branch change data? Is this coming from a specific API endpoint or state management system?",
    "Where in the codebase should I look to understand how 'Branch Changes' section is currently rendered (if at all)?",
    "Is there an existing implementation that shows similar information for other features that I can reference?",
    "What test cases exist for branch change detection and display that could help verify this fix?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host documentation",
    "Issue #316784 - Branch Changes display bug report"
  ]
}
```
